steps.title: Convert g-values into f-values
steps.content:
Do not calculate $g(x)$ directly, because the question asks for the $y$-intercept of $f$. Since $g(x)=\frac{f(x)}{x+3}$, multiply each $g(x)$ value by $x+3$:
  • $f(-27)=3(-24)=-72$
  • $f(-9)=0(-6)=0$
  • $f(21)=5(24)=120$

steps.title: Find f from the points
steps.content:
Use the two converted points $(-27,-72)$ and $(-9,0)$ to find the line for $f$:
$$m=\frac{0-(-72)}{-9-(-27)}=\frac{72}{18}=4$$
Substitute $(-9,0)$ into $f(x)=4x+b$: $$0=4(-9)+b,$$ so $b=36$. Therefore $$f(x)=4x+36.$$

steps.title: Answer the intercept question
steps.content: The $y$-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is $(0,b)$, so it is $(0,36)$. That matches Choice A — $\left(0,36\right)$.
